reasoning_content:
The score is 98.75/100.00, which is very good! The main issue seems to be with the StartOfLine detection for tokens that are not at the beginning of a line. Looking at the error, it seems like some tokens are missing the [StartOfLine] flag when they should have it.

Let me check the expected output for 001_var_defn.sysu.c to see what's expected:

```
int 'int'	 [StartOfLine]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:1>
identifier 'a'	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:5>
equal '='	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:7>
numeric_constant '3'	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:9>
semi ';'		Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:2:10>
int 'int'	 [StartOfLine]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:3:1>
identifier 'b'	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:3:5>
equal '='	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:3:7>
numeric_constant '5'	 [LeadingSpace]	Loc=</workspace/YatCC/test/cases/functional-0/001_var_defn.sysu.c:3:9>


```

I notice the semi token lacks the [LeadingSpace] flag, which is consistent with the expected output. The lexer seems to be correctly handling whitespace and line positioning for most tokens.

The key observation is that the first token on each line gets [StartOfLine], while subsequent tokens on the same line get [LeadingSpace] if there's whitespace before them. This pattern matches the expected token formatting.

I'll verify the implementation details to ensure the lexer is working as intended.
